title: Evil Eye, Jinn Possession, and Mental Health Issues - An Islamic Perspective
book: Evil Eye, Jinn Possession, and Mental Health Issues - An Islamic Perspective

node_types.description: A person receiving mental health care, with cultural, religious, and psychosocial context relevant to Islamic perspectives.

properties.name: cultural_identity
properties.description: Patient's cultural background and identity

properties.name: religious_beliefs
properties.description: Patient's Islamic beliefs and practices

properties.name: language
properties.description: Primary language(s) spoken by the patient

properties.name: family_dynamics
properties.description: Relevant family structure and relationships

properties.name: help_seeking_behavior
properties.description: Patterns and preferences for seeking help

properties.name: stigma_experience
properties.description: Experience of stigma related to mental health

properties.name: acculturation_level
properties.description: Degree of adaptation to non-Islamic cultures

properties.name: psychosocial_stressors
properties.description: Social, cultural, or religious stressors impacting mental health

supported_by_chapters: Part III Prevention, therapeutic and spiritual interventions

node_types.description: A mental health disorder or presenting problem, including both biomedical and Islamic explanatory models.

properties.name: diagnostic_label
properties.description: Clinical or cultural diagnosis (e.g., depression, Jinn possession)

properties.name: symptoms
properties.description: Reported symptoms and manifestations

properties.name: onset_context
properties.description: Context or triggers for onset

properties.name: perceived_cause
properties.description: Patient's or family's explanatory model (e.g., evil eye, spiritual, medical)

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Severity or impact of the issue

supported_by_chapters: Part II Evil eye and Possession Syndrome
Part III Prevention, therapeutic and spiritual interventions

node_types.description: A religious or spiritual concept relevant to mental health, such as evil eye, Jinn, Ruqyah, Tawheed, or Waswâs.

properties.name: concept_name
properties.description: Name of the Islamic concept

properties.name: definition
properties.description: Definition and explanation

properties.name: quranic_basis
properties.description: Qur’anic verses supporting the concept

properties.name: hadith_basis
properties.description: Hadith references supporting the concept

properties.name: cultural_variants
properties.description: Names or forms in different cultures/languages

supported_by_chapters: Part II Evil eye and Possession Syndrome

node_types.description: A therapeutic, spiritual, or preventive intervention, including Islamic and biomedical approaches.

properties.name: intervention_type
properties.description: Type of intervention (e.g., Ruqyah, CBT, medication, Dhikr)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Details of the intervention

properties.name: islamic_basis
properties.description: Islamic justification or source, if applicable

properties.name: evidence_base
properties.description: Evidence or rationale for use

properties.name: contraindications
properties.description: Risks or situations where not recommended

node_types.description: A person providing healing or therapeutic services, including Imams, faith healers (Raki), and mental health professionals.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role or title (e.g., Imam, Raki, psychiatrist)

properties.name: qualification
properties.description: Relevant qualifications or training

properties.name: approach
properties.description: Therapeutic or spiritual approach used

properties.name: cultural_alignment
properties.description: Degree of alignment with Islamic practices

supported_by_chapters: Part III Prevention, therapeutic and spiritual interventions

node_types.description: A risk, barrier, or challenge in the context of Islamic mental health care.

properties.name: risk_type
properties.description: Type of risk or challenge (e.g., misdiagnosis, stigma, exploitation)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Details of the risk or challenge

properties.name: affected_population
properties.description: Who is affected by this risk

node_types.description: A factor that promotes mental health or resilience, especially from an Islamic perspective.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Details of the protective factor

properties.name: islamic_basis
properties.description: Islamic justification or source, if applicable

node_types.description: A cultural practice or tradition relevant to mental health, including both Islamic and local customs.
